messages.content:
Consider the following variable declarations and initializations.

int a = 2;

int b = 6;

int c = 3;

Which of the following expressions evaluates to false ?

Responses

a < b == c < b

a < b == c < b

a > b == b < c

a > b == b < c

a < b != b < c

a < b != b < c

a < b != c < b

a < b != c < b

a > b != b > c
The expression "a < b != c < b" evaluates to false.

Consider the following code segment.

boolean a = true;

boolean b = false;

System.out.print((a == !b) != false);

What is printed as a result of executing this code segment?

Responses

false

false

true

true

0

0

1

1

Nothing is printed because the expression (a == !b) != false is an invalid parameter to the System.out.print method.
The output of executing this code segment would be "true".

Consider the following expression.

(3 + 4 == 5) != (3 + 4 >= 5)

What value, if any, does the expression evaluate to?

Responses

true

true

false

false

5

5

7

7

No value; relational operators cannot be used on arithmetic expressions.
The expression (3 + 4 == 5) != (3 + 4 >= 5) evaluates to false.
The value of total after executing this code segment would be 68.
The values of a, b, and c after executing this code segment would be: a = 0, b = 4, and c = 0
As a result of executing the code segment, the value "11" will be printed.

The test cases "temp = 30" and "temp = 51" can be used to show that the code does not work as intended. 

So the correct answer is: I and II only.

The values of num, min, and max that can be used to show that the code does not work as intended are:
num = 30, min = 20, max = 40.

So the correct answer is: num = 30, min = 20, max = 40.

The values of a, b, and c for which the code segment does not print the correct value are:

a = 3, b = 2, c = 1.

So the correct answer is: a = 3, b = 2, c = 1.

The code segment that is equivalent to the given code segment is:

if (a != b) { a++; } else if (a < b) { a++; } else { b++; }

II.

int result = (x > y) ? (x - y) : (y - x);

System.out.print(result);

Which of the following statements is true about the two code segments?

Responses

Both code segments are equivalent; they will produce the same output for any values of x and y.

Both code segments are equivalent except that the code segment labeled I also assigns a value to the variable result.

Both code segments are equivalent except that the code segment labeled II also assigns a value to the variable result.

Both code segments are equivalent except that the code segment labeled II uses the ternary conditional operator.

The code segments are not equivalent; they will produce different output for some values of x and y.

The correct response is: Both code segments are equivalent; they will produce the same output for any values of x and y.
